steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which statement is best supported by the passage. The key claim is that Azara's night monkeys forage more with higher lunar intensity because of their greater reliance on vision — meaning any cost of extra light is outweighed by the visual benefit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'their behavior should be more similar to that of European badgers': the passage explicitly explains the discrepancy, so similarity is not predicted by OFT here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'advantages likely shared by some other species not heavily reliant on vision': the passage ties the advantage specifically to vision reliance, ruling this out. ✗
  • Choice C — 'if increased lunar intensity imposed the same costs on the monkeys ... there would be no association': the passage does not establish what would happen under that hypothetical; a positive effect could still exist. ✗
  • Choice D — 'if increased lunar intensity creates any disadvantages ... those disadvantages are more than compensated for': the monkeys forage more with more light, so net benefits must exceed any costs — directly supported by the positive association and OFT logic. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
